title: sphalanthus littoreus
intro: Sphalanthus littoreus, also known as sea lavender or marsh sea lavender, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Lamiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to help in treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The health benefits of Sphalanthus littoreus include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
